rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PETERSON et al VS. AC AND S, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Itt Corporations Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 10, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT ITT CORPORATION'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying ITT Corporation's Motion For Summary Judgment. The "newly" proffered evidence was available to defendant when it filed its original motion. Given that this motion is based on the same grounds as before, defendant fails to meet the test for reconsideration (CCP 437 c(f)). =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ERICKA APPS VS. THELEN, REID,BROWN,RAYSMAN & STEINER LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 10, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANTS THELEN, REID, BROWN, RAYSMAN & STEINER LLP AND MARIO ALVARADO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff does not oppose the motion as to the second, third and sixth causes of action. As to the first and fifth causes of action, defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence; defendant fails to shift the burden of producing evidence as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue of fact as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first, second, third and sixth causes of action. Plaintiff's evidence fails to demonstrate that Mr. Alvarado's conduct constituted a sexual battery as alleged in the first cause of action. Plaintiff's concerns of retaliation, evidence that Mr. Alvarado was in fact plaintiff's manager to some degree, coupled with the lack of evidence as to Ms. Assefa following up on plaintiff's complaint and the inference that Mr. Alvarado was promoted create a factual record sufficient to defeat the motion as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC., A DELAWARE VS. JOHN FRANKLIN HEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By Plaintiff California Mortgage And Realty, Inc. Motion Will Be Heard In Dept. 302. Judge Busch Recused Himself From The Case.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 10, 2008, line 15. PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Power's Reply Declaration establishes that 21Mira Mesa, LLC is the owner of the loan and that CMR is the manager of 21 Mira Mesa, LLC and has "the power to do any and all acts necessary or convenient to or for the furtherance of the purposes of the Company" and shall have "the power to take all actions permitted of managers of limited liablility companies." What is not clear is whether, as a matter of law, CMR may maintain this action in its own name. It is the Court's conclusion that the Papillon Trust is a business trust (see In re Sung Soo Rim Irrevocable Intervivos Trust Bkrtcy, 177 B.R. 673 (1995) and the reasoning of The Cadle Company II v. Harvey (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 927 is not applicable to a business trust. Guaranty No.2 is valid and enforcible (see Mariners Savinings & Loan Ass'n v. Neil (1971) 22 Cal.App.3d 232) if plaintiff has standing, an issue raised by the second and sixth affimative defenses. Plaintiff relies on Hein's failure to respond to discovery to shift the burden of proof as to each of the affirmative defenses. This is not sufficient under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. The Court denies summary adjudication of the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and eighth defenses for that reason. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the seventh affirmative defense for plaintiff shifted the burden of producing evidence and defendant has not presented evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PM)

rulings.Case Title: INNOVATIVE DESIGN ENGINEERING ANIMATION VS. JAMES A. PHILLIPSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defts' Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner Or In The Alternative For An Order To Pot Bond By Date Certain; Request For Attorney Fees;

RESPONDENT JAMES PHILLIPSEN'S Motion To Dismiss Defendants' Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner Or In The Alternative For An Order To Post Bond By Date Certain; Request For Attorney Fees is off calendar - insufficient notice. Per CCP 1005(b) 16 Court days notice must be provided. Plaintiff neglected to account for the labor day holiday. =(302/PJM)
